summary: Keyword co-occurrence in bundled CLI lacks corroborating evidence of exfiltration
details: The package's bundled CLI (dist/cli.cjs and dist/cli.js) contains the tokens 'ping' and 'POST' on nearby lines, which triggered a network/command pattern rule. No traced code path demonstrates that these tokens are wired into an exfiltration mechanism: there is no evidence of an attacker-controlled destination, no env-var or credential scraping, and no install-time lifecycle hook that automatically invokes the flagged code. The keyword co-occurrence in a minified/bundled CLI is consistent with normal CLI tooling (e.g., a 'ping' subcommand and POST-based API calls). Routing for human review to inspect the bundle and confirm whether the POST destinations are attacker-controlled or legitimate.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
